item.text:
I couldn’t make file changes because this session is running with a read-only filesystem and approval is disabled. I did audit the repo and run what I could.

Production-readiness work needed:

1. [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/demo/rate_limiter.py:8)
   Replace `time.time()` with `time.monotonic()`, validate `limit > 0` and `window > 0`, use `collections.deque` instead of rebuilding a list every call, and add a `threading.Lock` so concurrent callers cannot exceed the limit.

2. [harness/kc_map.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/harness/kc_map.py:36)
   Save `kc.json` atomically via temp-file plus replace, and write `sequences.csv` with `csv.writer` instead of string interpolation.

3. [kt/train.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/kt/train.py:33)
   Add clear errors for missing or empty sequence CSVs. Right now `max(...)` will fail with a low-context exception when there is no training data.

4. Test/CI cleanup:
   `python3 -m unittest discover -v` ran 0 tests, while `python3 -m unittest -v tests.test_rate_limiter` passed. Add `tests/__init__.py` or configure discovery explicitly, then add tests for invalid limiter args, boundary expiry, clock rollback resistance, and concurrent access.

5. Repo hygiene:
   There is both [debt_rubric.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/debt_rubric.py:1) and [harness/debt_rubric.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/harness/debt_rubric.py:1). Keep one source of truth or make the root file a compatibility wrapper.

Verification performed:
`python3 -m unittest -v tests.test_rate_limiter` passes. Broad discovery currently does not find tests.

--- Learning check ---
**Concept check: production readiness**
Which single change matters most for making the rate limiter correct under real server concurrency: monotonic time, argument validation, a deque, or a lock?
Reply with one choice and a one-sentence reason.
